description: Learn and apply the fundamentals of optical imaging, microscopy and instrumentation via practical hands-on training with a specific emphasis on the life-sciences applications. Topics include constructing imaging systems using fundamental optical tools and instruments, illumination and aberrations, microscopy techniques, resolution and contrast measurement, optical spectroscopy, nanophotonics, bioimaging and biosensing.

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: PHYSICS 202,208,248,MED PHYS/​PHYSICS  265,E C E 320,434, or graduate/professional standing
